title: HELL NO ON THE TUNDRA
owner_override: Owner directed the story (North Slope data center, 500+ comments vs fewer than a dozen) and a COMEDIC register: an angry parka'd Alaskan yelling HELL NO, gigglable gags. Distinct treatment from the sober 2026-07-14 dispatch on the same proposal; pegged to the July 17 comment deadline (closes tomorrow).
concept: The AI data-center boom shows up on the North Slope with confident, unsettled numbers, and Alaska's public overwhelmingly says no. Told as a comedy: a greedy server-machine reaches for a square mile of tundra and gets buried in public comments, then revealed as an unbuilt PROPOSAL whose own price won't settle. The public comment window is the real vote, and it closes tomorrow.

treatment.logline: A hungry data-center machine reaches its power plug toward Alaska's North Slope and gets buried alive under 500-plus angry public comments before the July 17 window slams shut.
treatment.graft: Comedian spine (greedy machine, HELL NO, plug-morph, price flip-flop, paper storm, PROPOSAL-cutout reveal, LAST CALL calendar) + Explainer's scale-stack revelation (one building outdraws all urban Alaska by +30%) + Dramatist's mailbox-as-the-real-vote button and July 17 guillotine deadline.
treatment.judge_reasoning: Owner gave explicit creative direction (comedy, HELL NO, giggles) which functions as the judge's mandate; the Explainer's +30% scale-stack was grafted as the hardest-hitting revelation and the Dramatist's mailbox button as the loop-closing image.

fingerprint.motion: reach-then-bury, snap-zoom on the HELL NO
fingerprint.palette: state-navy burst + ember-red for the money, orange parkas on sage tundra, amber stat-bursts, signal-red LAST CALL
fingerprint.metaphor: the AI buildout drawn as a hungry machine whose own numbers will not settle, checked by a paper-flood of public comment and a mailbox that is the real vote

beats.vo: A machine got hungry. And it looked at Alaska.

draw.subject: greedy server-machine with hungry eyes + drool
draw.action: eyes Alaska and the North Slope pin

beats.vo: It wants a square mile of the North Slope near Deadhorse. Plus a gigawatt of gas power.

draw.subject: machine + plug
draw.action: reaches plug toward the pin, plug bulks to GIGAWATT

beats.vo: One building. About thirty percent more electricity than every city in Alaska, combined.

draw.subject: two bars
draw.action: data-center bar towers over all-urban-Alaska bar, casts a shadow

beats.vo: The cost, by the company's own math? Five hundred million dollars. Or over ten billion. A twenty times guess.

draw.subject: seesaw
draw.action: rocks and will not settle between the two numbers

beats.vo: So Alaskans grabbed a pen. Over five hundred comments. Fewer than a dozen said yes.

draw.subject: crowd of parka'd everyresidents
draw.action: point and cross arms as a paper storm flies
draw.annotation: 500+ PUBLIC COMMENTS / FEWER THAN A DOZEN IN FAVOR

beats.vo: Some kept it short. Quote: hell no. Quote: no A.I.

draw.subject: furious parka'd Alaskan
draw.action: bellows HELL NO, snap-zoom on the face
draw.annotation: HELL NO / NO AI (real public comments)

beats.vo: Here's the honest part. Nothing is built. No customers. No chips. The state has not decided.

draw.subject: machine redrawn as a dashed ghost
draw.action: PROPOSAL stamp thuds down, empty chip/customer slots
draw.annotation: PROPOSAL / NO CONFIRMED CHIPS / NO CUSTOMERS / STATE HAS NOT DECIDED

beats.means: the honest caveat, drawn as the loudest element

beats.vo: The comment window is the real vote. And it closes tomorrow. July seventeenth.

draw.subject: wall calendar
draw.action: flips to JULY 17, LAST CALL banner unrolls, clock ticks
draw.annotation: JULY 17 / 4:30 PM AKDT / ALASKA DNR / ADL 422741

beats.vo: So the loudest thing on the tundra is not the machine. It's the mailbox. What would your comment say?

draw.subject: overstuffed mailbox + little parka'd Alaskan
draw.action: mailbox bulges with comments, ghost machine defeated behind, loop to HELL NO

hook.frame1: greedy server-machine eyeing Alaska under a shouty headline
hook.headline: The AI Boom Wants Alaska
hook.loopback: final HELL NO parka pose mirrors the cold-open menace

voice.license: gray-area for publish (interim; owner plans ElevenLabs clone)
voice.note: cloned voice shelved per owner; kokoro attempted per owner request but blocked by env egress policy (github 403 on its spaCy dep)

divergence_note: Same proposal as 2026-07-14 but a deliberately distinct COMEDIC register (owner-directed), a fresh live peg (July 17 deadline closes tomorrow), all-new 2.5D Remotion scenes, and a public-revolt frame rather than the sober institutional one.
